items.title: Measurement-based quantum computing and feedback
items.description: Continuous monitoring and real-time feedback for quantum state preparation and error suppression, with control protocols tailored to realistic noise models and hardware-specific decoherence mechanisms.

items.title: Geometric optimization on quantum state manifolds
items.description: Development of quantum control protocols that exploit geometric optimization on quantum state manifolds, with applications to quantum chemistry and fault-tolerant quantum computation (e.g., continuous error correction).

items.title: Quantum annealing and complexity
items.description: Analysis of adiabatic quantum annealing, its computational complexity and speedup prospects, and the design of control strategies for ground-state preparation on near-term hardware.

items.title: Stochastic thermodynamics for quantum resources
items.description: Using stochastic thermodynamics for resource estimation in quantum computing and for understanding energy–information tradeoffs in controlled open quantum systems.
